---
title: "estimate_layout_heights"
---

[< Back to Reference](../reference.qmd) | [View source](https://codeberg.org/usrbinr/stwd/src/branch/main/R/stwd.R#L1079)

# `estimate_layout_heights()`

Estimate Layout Heights for Text

## Usage

```r
estimate_layout_heights(
  title = NULL,
  subtitle = NULL,
  narrative = NULL,
  title_size = 12,
  subtitle_size = 10,
  narrative_size = 10,
  caption = NULL,
  narrative_width = 0.35,
  output_width = 12
)
```

## Arguments

| Argument | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `title` | Title text (or NULL). |
| `subtitle` | Subtitle text (or NULL). |
| `narrative` | Narrative text (or NULL). |
| `title_size` | Title font size in pts. Default: 16. |
| `subtitle_size` | Subtitle font size in pts. Default: 11. |
| `narrative_size` | Narrative font size in pts. Default: 10. |
| `caption` | Caption text (or NULL). |
| `narrative_width` | Width proportion for narrative (affects line wrapping). Default: 0.35. |
| `output_width` | Expected output width in inches. Default: 12. Larger widths mean fewer line wraps and smaller height requirements. |

## Value

A named list with recommended height proportions:

- `title_height`: Proportion for title (0-1)

- `subtitle_height`: Proportion for subtitle (0-1)

- `caption_height`: Proportion for caption (0-1)

- `content_height`: Remaining space for plot/narrative (0-1)

- `narrative_width`: Echo of input (for convenience)

## Description

Estimates appropriate height proportions for title, subtitle, and narrative
based on text length and font size. Use this to avoid text clipping.
This is called automatically by `story_layout()` when `auto_heights = TRUE`.
